title: APOE alleles and late-onset Alzheimer disease context
frequencyNote: APOE allele frequencies vary substantially by ancestry; frequency context should not be read as personal risk.
notes: ClinVar is used here as clinical context for a common risk-associated genotype pattern, not as a diagnostic assertion.
Interpretation requires both rs429358 and rs7412 and remains unphased in consumer-array data.

title: F5 Factor V Leiden
frequencyNote: The Leiden allele is uncommon overall and varies by population; frequency does not measure personal clotting risk.
notes: ClinVar treats this as a well-established thrombophilia variant.
Consumer-array positives should be confirmed clinically before action.

title: HFE C282Y and H63D common haemochromatosis variants
frequencyNote: HFE variant frequencies and penetrance vary; biochemical testing matters more than genotype alone.
notes: This pack only covers common consumer-array HFE markers.
Normal findings here do not exclude iron overload.

title: F2 prothrombin G20210A
frequencyNote: The A allele is relatively uncommon and its impact depends on personal and family clotting history.
notes: Use as thrombophilia context only; it does not replace clinical assessment.
Absence in uploaded chip data does not rule out inherited clotting disorders.

title: CYP2C19 and clopidogrel CPIC context
notes: A single *2 tagging marker is not a full CPIC diplotype call.
Medication changes require clinical review and confirmatory testing.

title: NUDT15 thiopurine-response CPIC context
summary: NUDT15 rs116855232 is a pharmacogenomic marker relevant to thiopurine toxicity risk in CPIC context.
detail: This is not a full pharmacogene report. Thiopurine prescribing requires clinical PGx interpretation and medical supervision.
whyItMatters: Adding CPIC-backed PGx markers expands the local database while keeping medication warnings prominent.
notes: Preview only; do not change medication based on this report.
Consumer arrays may not provide all alleles needed for a full CPIC phenotype.

title: SLCO1B1 and simvastatin myopathy CPIC context
notes: This marker is useful PGx context but not a complete statin safety report.
Drug selection and dosing belong in clinical care.

title: CYP2C9/VKORC1 warfarin CPIC context
notes: Consumer-array data is incomplete for warfarin dosing algorithms.
Clinical factors and formal dosing guidance are required.

title: gnomAD population-frequency context for common HFE variants
frequencyNote: gnomAD is used only to explain population frequency and rarity, not to decide clinical significance.
notes: Frequency annotations are supportive context.
Frequencies vary by ancestry and cohort composition.

title: ABCC11 earwax and body-odour marker
summary: ABCC11 rs17822931 is a well-known common trait marker linked to wet versus dry earwax and related apocrine-gland traits.
detail: This is a low-stakes trait association. Deana reports it as phenotype context only, not as health guidance.
whyItMatters: This marker is commonly present on consumer arrays and is useful for expanding local trait coverage beyond the small hand-authored seed set.
notes: Trait expression can vary and should be treated as a tendency, not a certainty.
This record is safe to match locally because it does not require a live marker-specific search.

title: ADH1B alcohol-metabolism context
summary: ADH1B rs1229984 is a common alcohol-metabolism marker often studied alongside drinking behaviour and alcohol-response traits.
detail: This is association context only. Personal behaviour, environment, and other genes are central.
whyItMatters: It adds local metabolic context from public GWAS/literature sources without sending the marker to a live search API.
notes: Association context only.
Do not use for medical or behavioural recommendations.

title: ALDH2 alcohol-flush context
summary: ALDH2 rs671 is associated with reduced aldehyde dehydrogenase activity and alcohol-flush response in many carriers.
detail: This can be relevant to alcohol tolerance, but it should not be used as permission to drink or as medical advice.
whyItMatters: ALDH2 is a high-signal metabolic marker that appears in many consumer datasets.
notes: Alcohol response is influenced by behavior and other biology.
Health-risk interpretation requires more than this local marker card.

title: BDNF Val66Met literature context
summary: BDNF rs6265 is a frequently studied neurobiology marker with mixed literature across cognition, mood, and exercise-response topics.
detail: This card is intentionally broad and conservative. It does not predict mental health, cognition, or training response.
whyItMatters: It gives Deana local coverage for a marker users often see elsewhere while avoiding deterministic claims.
release: PubMed citation metadata, queried during pack build
notes: Associations vary by study design and population.
Use only as research context.

title: CYP1A2 caffeine metabolism association
notes: Association context is useful for lifestyle guidance but should defer to lived response.
This should not be presented as medical advice.

title: COMT Val158Met literature context
summary: COMT rs4680 is a heavily studied dopamine-metabolism marker with mixed and often overinterpreted associations.
detail: Deana includes this as literature context only. Behavioural and mental-health claims around this marker are often overstated.
whyItMatters: This marker is commonly requested by users, so including a cautious local card is better than leaving it entirely to SNPedia.
release: PubMed citation metadata, queried during pack build
notes: This marker has mixed literature and should not be interpreted deterministically.
No mental-health or medication conclusion should be drawn from this single marker.

title: ANKK1/DRD2 dopamine literature context
summary: ANKK1/DRD2 rs1800497 is widely discussed in dopamine and reward-related literature, with mixed interpretation quality.
detail: This local card exists to provide cautious context and avoid overclaiming from a single common marker.
whyItMatters: It is a common consumer-DNA discussion marker that benefits from Deana's conservative framing.
release: PubMed citation metadata, queried during pack build
notes: Do not infer personality, addiction risk, or treatment response from this marker alone.
This is a literature-context card, not a clinical interpretation.

title: HERC2/OCA2 eye colour association
notes: GWAS Catalog context supports this as a high-signal common trait marker.
Eye colour remains polygenic and cannot be fully predicted from one marker.

title: FTO body-weight association context
summary: FTO rs9939609 is a widely replicated common-variant association for body weight and BMI-related traits.
detail: The effect of one common variant is small. Diet, activity, sleep, medication, environment, and many other loci matter more than this marker alone.
whyItMatters: This expands local GWAS coverage while keeping common-variant effect sizes in context.
notes: Common-variant association only; not a diagnosis or prediction.
Single-marker weight associations should be presented with extra caution.

title: HLA celiac-disease tag marker context
summary: rs2187668 is an HLA-region tag marker associated with celiac disease susceptibility in GWAS literature.
detail: This is susceptibility context only. It cannot diagnose celiac disease and does not replace antibody testing, biopsy, or clinical evaluation.
whyItMatters: HLA-region markers can add useful local context, but they need very strong caveats in a consumer-array report.
notes: Association context only, not diagnosis.
HLA tagging can vary by ancestry and array design.

title: LCT/MCM6 lactase persistence association
frequencyNote: Lactase persistence alleles differ sharply across populations due to recent selection.
notes: This is one of the cleaner consumer-array trait examples.
Real-world lactose tolerance still depends on diet, gut context, and symptoms.

title: MC1R pigmentation context
summary: MC1R rs1805007 is one of several MC1R variants associated with pigmentation traits such as red hair tendency and sun sensitivity context.
detail: Pigmentation is polygenic. This local marker card should not be used as a skin-cancer risk assessment.
whyItMatters: MC1R variants are common high-interest appearance markers and useful additions to the local trait database.
notes: Trait context only.
Skin cancer risk depends on many genetic and environmental factors.

title: OXTR social-behaviour literature context
summary: OXTR rs53576 appears in social-behaviour literature, but reported effects are mixed and context-dependent.
detail: This is included only as a cautious literature card. It should not be used to label personality or social ability.
whyItMatters: SNPedia often surfaces behavioural markers; Deana's local database should cover them with stronger caveats.
release: PubMed citation metadata, queried during pack build
notes: Behavioural genetics findings can be fragile and population-specific.
This card is intentionally conservative.

title: TAS2R38 bitter-taste sensitivity
summary: TAS2R38 rs713598 is part of the common bitter-taste haplotype often discussed for PTC/PROP tasting.
detail: This marker contributes to bitter-taste sensitivity but is best interpreted with nearby TAS2R38 markers and real-world taste experience.
whyItMatters: Taste markers are high-interest, low-risk findings that can be included locally with conservative wording.
notes: This is trait context only.
A full TAS2R38 haplotype is more informative than one marker.

title: ACTN3 R577X performance literature context
release: PubMed citation metadata, queried during pack build
notes: Included as an interesting trait with explicit low-certainty wording.
Training, physiology, and environment dominate over this single marker.

title: MTHFR C677T folate and homocysteine literature context
release: PubMed citation metadata, queried during pack build
notes: Stored as citation context only; no live PubMed search is run against the user's markers.
This marker is common and often overinterpreted, so reporting must stay conservative.
